title: fix: exclude 4xx from model monitor request counts and success rate
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Fairer grades for models
gist.blurb: We tweaked the model monitor so user errors no longer drag down a model's success rate. Now you get a much clearer picture of actual system performance!
gist.summary: Updated the model monitor to exclude 4xx client errors from request counts and success rate calculations.
gist.impact: Model success rates now accurately reflect system reliability rather than being skewed by bad requests or auth errors.
gist.visual_concept: The model monitor is about tracking stats and numbers. The Monitor Robot displaying a clean, accurate chart on its screen, while Polly the bee sweeps away little '4xx' pixel dust bunnies (representing the excluded user errors).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny maid headband, sweeping away tiny '400' and '404' pixel dust bunnies with a little broom. Next to her, a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head displays a bright, upward-trending chart on its screen. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
